description:
## Goal

Introduce the per-heap ProcBin object kind and the MSO list that tracks it. Wire the post-Cheney sweep that updates surviving entries and releases dead ones.

This ticket combines ProcBin allocation, MSO bookkeeping, and the GC sweep because each is incoherent without the others: allocating ProcBins without sweeping leaves dangling entries after GC; sweeping without ProcBins has nothing to sweep.

## Depends on

fz-cty.2 (SharedBin allocator).

## Layout

ProcBin: 32 bytes total.
```
offset 0..16  HeapHeader { kind=ProcBin, flags=0, size_bytes=32, schema_id=0, _reserved=0 }
offset 16..24 shared_ptr: *mut SharedBin
offset 24..32 padding (zero)
```

## Changes

### runtime/src/fz_value.rs
- Add `HeapKind::ProcBin = 10` to enum.
- Update `HeapKind::from_u16` for value 10.

### runtime/src/heap.rs
- Add `pub mso_list: Vec<*mut HeapHeader>` field on `Heap`. Initialise empty in `Heap::new`.
- Add `Heap::alloc_procbin(shared_ptr: *mut SharedBin) -> *mut HeapHeader`:
  - Allocate 32 bytes via existing `alloc()`.
  - Write header (kind=ProcBin, size_bytes=32, others zero).
  - Write shared_ptr at offset 16.
  - Push returned pointer onto `self.mso_list`.
  - Caller is responsible for the refcount already being correctly established before the call.
- Add `cheney_trace_children` match arm for `HeapKind::ProcBin`: no-op (no FzValue children).
- Add MSO sweep in `gc_with_extra_roots`, AFTER the BFS scan loop and BEFORE the `pool_free` of the old `block_start` and `abandoned_blocks`. For each entry in `mso_list`:
  - Read header kind at offset 0 of the from-space entry.
  - If `FORWARDED_KIND`: read to-space pointer at offset 8; replace the entry with that pointer.
  - Else: read shared_ptr at offset 16; call `shared_bin_release`; mark entry for removal.
  - Use `Vec::retain_mut` (or rebuild) to filter dead entries.
- Update `Heap::drop`:
  - Before returning blocks to the pool, walk `mso_list` and release every entry's shared_ptr. The ProcBin objects themselves are inside the heap blocks about to be reclaimed; their shared refs would otherwise leak.

### runtime/src/heap.rs — deep_copy_value
- This ticket does NOT add the ProcBin arm to deep_copy_value (that is .4). Without that arm, calling deep_copy_value on a value graph containing a ProcBin will panic via the existing "invalid HeapKind" check. Acceptable: no caller produces ProcBins yet in this ticket.

## Tests

- alloc_procbin pushes the new ptr onto mso_list; header has kind=ProcBin, size_bytes=32; shared_ptr round-trips through offset 16.
- ProcBin survives Cheney: build a rooted reference graph that includes a ProcBin reachable from `parked_cont`. Run GC. Assert: mso_list now contains the to-space pointer; shared-bin live count unchanged.
- ProcBin dies in Cheney: allocate ProcBin but do not root it. Run GC. Assert: mso_list is empty post-sweep; shared-bin live count dropped by 1; refcount of the SharedBin was decremented (and freed if it was 1).
- Cheney sweep ordering: covered implicitly by the above tests — if pool_free ran before the sweep, the from-space reads would be UAF and tests would fail under MIRI/ASAN-style runs.
- Heap::drop releases all live ProcBin shared refs (allocate two ProcBins pointing to distinct SharedBins, drop the heap, assert shared-bin live count is zero).

## Verification

- All new tests pass.
- Existing GC tests untouched.
- Three-path parity: not exercised yet; codegen unchanged in this ticket.
title: fz-shr.3: HeapKind::ProcBin, MSO list, and post-Cheney sweep
